name: Claude Jones
abstract: Claude Jones (February 11, 1901 – January 17, 1962) was an American jazz trombonist.

works.t: Black, Brown and Beige: Come Sunday

about_quotes.speaker: Monk Montgomery
about_quotes.evidence: I met the great Claude Jones... And I was also in the band there... we went to Wilberforce together... they hired me to play the first alto. And I stayed two years in the band.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Claude Jones, that's it, yeah. Claude was very -- marvelous trombone player... But they would put me on... these guys were so full of fun... I was like a youngster in the band

about_quotes.speaker: Hinton Milt
about_quotes.evidence: Claude Jones the great trombone player and Kay Johnson, they go to Cab... 'That kid's been playing in the band two months now.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Hinton Milt — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Doc Cheatham
about_quotes.evidence: One day Claude Jones did it, though. Claude Jones, he could actually pass. Claude Jones did it -- we were side-tracked into this -- right across, old beat grease joint, Claude Jones, went on in the restaurant, sat down at the counter and they served him.

about_quotes.speaker: Horace Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: We had three trombonists -- Claude Jones, a guy named Freddie Robinson... Claude Jones was one of the trombone players who later was with Fletcher's band too.

about_quotes.speaker: Quentin Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: Claude Jones was playing with the band at the time... And Claude Jones was playing with the band at the time and he finally married my sister.
